summary: Linked the fighter translation unit `melee/ft/ft_0819.c` as matching by removing its hand-written assembly object from the build and switching build configuration to compile the C source. The PR also adjusted nearby `.sdata2` symbol names/scopes in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, replacing several generated `ft_804D83A0`/`it_804DD9E8`-style names with local compiler-style `@NNN` constants. There was no PR body or review discussion in the provided slice, so rationale beyond enabling the C object to link as matching is inferred only from the diff.
what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/ft/ft_0819.s`, a 167-line assembly fallback containing `ft_80081938`, `ft_800819A8`, `ft_80081A00`, and local `.sdata2` floats at `0x804D83A0`/`0x804D83A4`.
Changed `obj_files.mk` so the linked object switched from `$(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/ft_0819.s.o` to `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ft_0819.c.o`.
Updated `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` to make `0x804D83A0` and `0x804D83A4` local `@253`/`@254` float constants instead of named `ft_804D83A0`/`ft_804D83A4`.
Updated item `.sdata2` entries at `0x804DD9E8` through `0x804DDA00` from global `it_804DD9E8`-style names to local `@206`, `@207`, `@212`, `@231`, and `@232` constants.
smart_moves: Performed the standard full link handoff for a matched unit: delete the asm fallback, mark the C file `Matching`, and point `obj_files.mk` at the source object.
Kept symbol-map changes in the same PR as the link switch, which is important when local float/double constants are emitted by the matched C object rather than by the old assembly labels.
Used local `@NNN` naming for compiler-emitted constants instead of preserving artificial address-derived globals, reducing unnecessary exported/global-looking symbols in `symbols.txt`.
decomp_lessons: When a file becomes matching, update both the project configuration (`configure.py`) and the make object list (`obj_files.mk`); changing only one leaves the asm object or nonmatching status in place.
Assembly deletion is a useful signal that the C source now owns all emitted text/data for that translation unit, including local `.sdata2` constants.

For matched C output, local literals in `.sdata2` should generally be represented as local `@NNN` symbols when that matches compiler-generated symbol behavior, not as invented `ft_804D...` or `it_804D...` global names.
Nearby symbol-map entries outside the directly linked file may need correction when section layout or literal ownership becomes clearer during matching.
naming_conventions: Use `ft_####.c` / `ft_####.s` filenames for unnamed fighter translation units by address range, here `ft_0819`.
Use `ft_80081938`, `ft_800819A8`, and `ft_80081A00` for still-unnamed global functions identified by address.
Use compiler-style local labels such as `@253`, `@254`, `@206`, `@207`, `@212`, `@231`, and `@232` for local float/double constants in `symbols.txt`.
Avoid address-derived global-looking names such as `ft_804D83A0` or `it_804DD9E8` when the symbol is actually local constant data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted `ft_0819` from asm-backed to C-backed linking by replacing `asm/melee/ft/ft_0819.s.o` with `src/melee/ft/ft_0819.c.o`.
Marked the object as `Matching` in `configure.py`, indicating the existing C source matched sufficiently to replace the assembly fallback.

The removed assembly shows the matched unit covered three text functions and two local `.sdata2` float constants, so future validation for similar link PRs should check both text and small-data output.
Symbol-map scope/name adjustments for `.sdata2` literals were part of the matching/linking work, suggesting literal labels can be a blocker or cleanup step for successful exact linking.
follow_up_queries: Find the source for `src/melee/ft/ft_0819.c` to study the C patterns that matched `ft_80081938`, `ft_800819A8`, and `ft_80081A00`.
Search later PRs for `ft_0819` to see whether the anonymous function names or struct fields around offsets `0x1930` through `0x1950` were renamed.
Search symbol-map history for `@253`/`@254` near `0x804D83A0` and item constants near `0x804DD9E8` to understand why those local literal names were required.
